| `sum-range-cube-mul-choose` вҖ” For every natural n, 8В·(sum of kВіВ·C(n,k) for k in 0..n) = nВІ(n+3)В·2вҒҝ; the third binomial moment вҲ‘kВіC(n,k) = nВІ(n+3)2^(nвҲ’3). | proved | 3 | вҖ” | classic identities (binomial-moment tower вҖ” compounds on the proved `sum-range-sq-mul-choose`) | The third moment of the binomial distribution scaled by 2вҒҝ; Graham, Knuth & Patashnik, Concrete Mathematics, Ch. 5 (binomial coefficients / generating-function moments); Riordan, Combinatorial Identities. |
| `sum-range-cube-odd` вҖ” For every natural n, the sum of the cubes of the first n odd numbers equals n^2 * (2n^2 - 1); i.e. 1^3 + 3^3 + ... + (2n-1)^3 = n^2(2n^2 - 1). | proved | 2 | вҖ” | classic identities | Standard finite-sum identity вҲ‘(2k-1)^3 = n^2(2n^2-1); CRC Standard Mathematical Tables (sums of powers of odd integers); Gradshteyn & Ryzhik, Table of Integrals, Series, and Products (sums section). |
| `sum-range-fall-mul-choose` вҖ” For every natural n, 4В·(sum of k(kвҲ’1)В·C(n,k) for k in 0..n) = n(nвҲ’1)В·2вҒҝ; the second falling-factorial moment вҲ‘k(kвҲ’1)C(n,k) = n(nвҲ’1)2^(nвҲ’2). | proved | 3 | вҖ” | classic identities (binomial-moment tower) | Second factorial moment of the binomial distribution; from double absorption k(kвҲ’1)C(n,k) = n(nвҲ’1)C(nвҲ’2,kвҲ’2). Graham, Knuth & Patashnik, Concrete Mathematics, Ch. 5. |
| `sum-range-fib-even-index` вҖ” For every natural n, вҲ‘_{i<n} F(2(i+1)) = F(2n+1) вҲ’ 1: the sum of the first n even-indexed Fibonacci numbers FвӮӮ + FвӮ„ + вӢҜ + F_{2n} equals F(2n+1) вҲ’ 1. | proved | 3 | вҖ” | Fibonacci identities | Vajda (1989), identity (6); Koshy, Thm 5.2. Companion to the odd-index sum `sum-range-fib-odd-index`, whose result it can reuse. |
| `sum-range-fib-odd-index` вҖ” For every natural n, вҲ‘_{i<n} F(2i+1) = F(2n): the sum of the first n odd-indexed Fibonacci numbers FвӮҒ + FвӮғ + вӢҜ + F_{2nвҲ’1} equals F(2n). | proved | 2 | вҖ” | Fibonacci identities | Vajda, Fibonacci & Lucas Numbers and the Golden Section (1989), identity (5); Koshy, Fibonacci and Lucas Numbers with Applications, Thm 5.1. mathlib has single-term `Nat.fib_two_mul` and the all-index `Nat.fib_succ_eq_succ_sum`, but no odd-indexed Fibonacci sum. |
| `sum-range-fib-sq` вҖ” For every natural n, the sum over i in 0..n of (fib i)^2 equals fib n * fib (n+1) (the telescoping identity F_0^2+...+F_n^2 = F_n F_{n+1}). | proved | 2 | [packet-ready](upstream/sum-range-fib-sq.md) | classic identities | Standard Fibonacci telescoping identity; Koshy, Fibonacci and Lucas Numbers with Applications, В§5; Vajda, Fibonacci & Lucas Numbers, and the Golden Section. |
| `sum-range-id-eq-choose-two` вҖ” For every natural n, вҲ‘_{i<n} i = C(n, 2): the Gauss sum 0 + 1 + вӢҜ + (nвҲ’1) equals the binomial coefficient 'n choose 2'. | proved | 2 | вҖ” | Gauss summation as a binomial coefficient | The handshake identity вҲ‘k = C(n,2); Graham, Knuth & Patashnik, Concrete Mathematics, В§1. mathlib has `Finset.sum_range_id` and `Nat.choose_two_right` as separate closed forms (both n(n-1)/2) but not the theorem equating them. |
| `sum-range-mul-two-pow` вҖ” For every natural n, the sum over i in 0..n-1 of i * 2^i equals (n - 2) * 2^n + 2 (stated over в„Ө, where the right side is negative-free for all n). | proved | 2 | вҖ” | classic identities | Arithmetico-geometric sum вҲ‘kВ·2^k = (n-2)В·2^n + 2; Graham, Knuth & Patashnik, Concrete Mathematics, В§2.5 (the perturbation-method worked example вҲ‘kВ·2^k). |
| `sum-range-odd-eq-sq` вҖ” For every natural n, the sum over i in 0..n-1 of (2i+1) equals n^2 (i.e. 1+3+5+...+(2n-1) = n^2). | proved | 1 | [packet-ready](upstream/sum-range-odd-eq-sq.md) | classic identities | Classical 'gnomon' identity (sum of consecutive odd numbers is a perfect square). Graham, Knuth & Patashnik, Concrete Mathematics, 2nd ed., В§2.5; Rosen, Discrete Mathematics and Its Applications, 7thвҖҰ |
| `sum-range-pentagonal-closed-form` вҖ” Closed form for the sum of the first n pentagonal numbers: $2 \sum_{k=0}^n \frac{3k^2-k}{2} = n^2(n+1)$. | proved | 3 | вҖ” | classic figural number identities | Pentagonal number theorem for partial sums of pentagonal numbers. |
| `sum-range-pow-five-add-pow-seven` вҖ” For every natural n, (sum of iвҒө for i in 0..n) + (sum of iвҒ· for i in 0..n) = 2В·(sum of i for i in 0..n)вҒҙ; i.e. вҲ‘kвҒө + вҲ‘kвҒ· = 2(вҲ‘k)вҒҙ = 2TвҒҙ where T = n(n+1)/2 is the n-th triangular number. | proved | 4 | вҖ” | classic identities (power-sum tower вҖ” the **crown**: compounds on `sum-range-pow-five-closed-form` + `sum-range-pow-seven-closed-form`) | A classic Faulhaber curiosity: the sum of the fifth- and seventh-power sums is exactly twice the fourth power of the triangular number, generalising Nicomachus's вҲ‘kВі = (вҲ‘k)ВІ = TВІ one octave up. Verified вҲҖ n; see Knuth, "Johann Faulhaber and sums of powers", Math. Comp. 61 (1993) for the triangular-number structure of odd-power sums. |
| `sum-range-pow-five-closed-form` вҖ” For every natural n, 12 * (sum of i^5 for i in 0..n) = n^2 (n+1)^2 (2n^2 + 2n - 1); Faulhaber's closed form for fifth powers, вҲ‘k^5 = (2n^6 + 6n^5 + 5n^4 - n^2)/12. | proved | 3 | вҖ” | classic identities | Faulhaber's formula, p = 5 case; Conway & Guy, The Book of Numbers (sums of powers); D. E. Knuth, "Johann Faulhaber and sums of powers", Math. Comp. 61 (1993); CRC Standard Mathematical Tables. |
| `sum-range-pow-five-faulhaber-triangular` вҖ” For every natural n, 3В·(sum of iвҒө for i in 0..n) = (sum of i for i in 0..n)ВІВ·(4В·(sum of i for i in 0..n)вҲ’1); i.e. вҲ‘kвҒө = TВІ(4TвҲ’1)/3 where T = вҲ‘k. Faulhaber's theorem made concrete: the fifth-power sum is a pure polynomial in the triangular number T. | proved | 3 | вҖ” | classic identities (Faulhaber-in-T tower вҖ” odd-power rung; compounds on `sum-range-pow-five-closed-form`) | Faulhaber's 1631 result that odd-power sums are polynomials in T = n(n+1)/2: вҲ‘kвҒө = (4TВівҲ’TВІ)/3 = TВІ(4TвҲ’1)/3. Knuth, "Johann Faulhaber and sums of powers", Math. Comp. 61 (1993). |
| `sum-range-pow-four-closed-form` вҖ” For every natural n, 30 * (sum of k^4 for k in 0..n) = n(n+1)(2n+1)(3n^2+3n-1), the integer (в„Ө) form of the Faulhaber p=4 identity. | proved | 2 | [packet-ready](upstream/sum-range-pow-four-closed-form.md) | classic identities | Faulhaber's formula, case p=4. Conway & Guy, The Book of Numbers, Ch. 2; Knuth, 'Johann Faulhaber and sums of powers', Math. Comp. 61 (1993). |
| `sum-range-pow-four-triangular-form` вҖ” For every natural n, 15В·(sum of iвҒҙ for i in 0..n) = (sum of i for i in 0..n)В·(2n+1)В·(3nВІ+3nвҲ’1); i.e. вҲ‘kвҒҙ = T(2n+1)(3nВІ+3nвҲ’1)/15 where T = вҲ‘k. | proved | 3 | вҖ” | classic identities (Faulhaber-in-T tower вҖ” even-power rung) | Faulhaber's theorem, even-power case: вҲ‘kвҒҙ = n(n+1)(2n+1)(3nВІ+3nвҲ’1)/30 = T(2n+1)(3nВІ+3nвҲ’1)/15. Knuth, "Johann Faulhaber and sums of powers", Math. Comp. 61 (1993); CRC Standard Mathematical Tables. |
| `sum-range-pow-seven-closed-form` вҖ” For every natural n, 24В·(sum of iвҒ· for i in 0..n) = nВІ(n+1)ВІ(3nвҒҙ+6nВівҲ’nВІвҲ’4n+2); Faulhaber's closed form for seventh powers, вҲ‘kвҒ· = (3nвҒё+12nвҒ·+14nвҒ¶вҲ’7nвҒҙ+2nВІ)/24. | proved | 4 | вҖ” | classic identities (power-sum tower вҖ” the harder odd-power rung) | Faulhaber's formula, p = 7; Conway & Guy, The Book of Numbers; D. E. Knuth, "Johann Faulhaber and sums of powers", Math. Comp. 61 (1993). Faulhaber's own result: odd-power sums are polynomials in the triangular number T = n(n+1)/2. |
| `sum-range-pow-seven-faulhaber-triangular` вҖ” For every natural n, 3В·(sum of iвҒ· for i in 0..n) = (sum of i for i in 0..n)ВІВ·(6В·(sum of i for i in 0..n)ВІвҲ’4В·(sum of i for i in 0..n)+1); i.e. вҲ‘kвҒ· = TВІ(6TВІвҲ’4T+1)/3 where T = вҲ‘k. The seventh-power sum as a pure polynomial in the triangular number. | proved | 4 | вҖ” | classic identities (Faulhaber-in-T tower вҖ” the **capstone** odd-power rung; compounds on `sum-range-pow-seven-closed-form`) | Faulhaber's theorem for p=7: вҲ‘kвҒ· = (6TвҒҙвҲ’4TВі+TВІ)/3 = TВІ(6TВІвҲ’4T+1)/3. Knuth, "Johann Faulhaber and sums of powers", Math. Comp. 61 (1993). |
| `sum-range-pow-six-closed-form` вҖ” For every natural n, 42В·(sum of iвҒ¶ for i in 0..n) = n(n+1)(2n+1)(3nвҒҙ+6nВівҲ’3n+1); Faulhaber's closed form for sixth powers, вҲ‘kвҒ¶ = (6nвҒ·+21nвҒ¶+21nвҒөвҲ’7nВі+n)/42. | proved | 3 | вҖ” | classic identities (power-sum tower вҖ” the next rung above proved p=2..p=5) | Faulhaber's formula, p = 6; Conway & Guy, The Book of Numbers (sums of powers); D. E. Knuth, "Johann Faulhaber and sums of powers", Math. Comp. 61 (1993); CRC Standard Mathematical Tables. |
| `sum-range-pronic` вҖ” For every natural n, 3 * (sum of i(i+1) for i in 0..n) = n(n+1)(n+2); the sum of the first pronic (oblong) numbers, equivalently 2 * C(n+2, 3). | proved | 2 | вҖ” | classic identities | Pronic/oblong number sums вҲ‘k(k+1) = n(n+1)(n+2)/3; Graham, Knuth & Patashnik, Concrete Mathematics, Ch. 2; CRC Standard Mathematical Tables (figurate numbers). |
| `sum-range-recip-pronic` вҖ” For every natural n, the sum over i in 0..n-1 of 1/((i+1)(i+2)) equals n/(n+1) (over в„ҡ); the classic telescoping sum 1/(1В·2) + 1/(2В·3) + ... + 1/(n(n+1)) = n/(n+1). | proved | 3 | вҖ” | classic identities | Classic telescoping/partial-fractions identity вҲ‘ 1/(k(k+1)) = n/(n+1); Graham, Knuth & Patashnik, Concrete Mathematics, В§2.5 (partial fractions); standard first-year analysis exercise. |
| `sum-range-recip-triangular` вҖ” For every natural n, вҲ‘_{k<n} 2/((k+1)(k+2)) = 2n/(n+1) over в„ҡ: the reciprocals of the triangular numbers telescope (вҲ‘ 1/T_k вҶ’ 2). | proved | 3 | вҖ” | telescoping series (reciprocals of triangular numbers) | Standard telescoping series; the sum of reciprocals of all triangular numbers is 2. Companion to `sum-range-recip-pronic` (1/(k(k+1))) already in the library, sharing the partial-fraction machinery over в„ҡ. |
| `sum-range-sq-closed-form` вҖ” For every natural n, 6 * (sum of i^2 for i in 0..n) = n(n+1)(2n+1), the integer form of 1^2+...+n^2 = n(n+1)(2n+1)/6. | proved | 2 | [packet-ready](upstream/sum-range-sq-closed-form.md) | classic identities | Faulhaber's formula, case p=2 (square pyramidal number). Apostol, Calculus Vol. 1, 2nd ed., В§I.4.2; Graham, Knuth & Patashnik, Concrete Mathematics, В§2.5; Avigad/Massot, Mathematics in Lean, В§5вҖҰ |
| `sum-range-sq-even` вҖ” For every natural n, 3 * (sum of (2i)^2 for i in 0..n-1) = 2n(n-1)(2n-1); the sum of the squares of the first n even numbers 0^2 + 2^2 + ... + (2n-2)^2 in closed form. | proved | 2 | вҖ” | classic identities | Even-square sums вҲ‘(2k)^2 = 2m(m+1)(2m+1)/3 (here reindexed from 0); CRC Standard Mathematical Tables (sums of powers); companion of the proved sum-range-sq-odd-closed-form. |
| `sum-range-sq-mul-choose` вҖ” Weighted sum of squares of binomial coefficients: $4 \sum_{k=0}^n k^2 \binom{n}{k} = n(n+1)2^n$. | proved | 3 | вҖ” | classic combinatorial identities | Standard combinatorial identity for the second moment of binomial coefficients, derived from differentiating the binomial theorem twice. |
| `sum-range-sq-odd-closed-form` вҖ” For every natural n, 3 * (sum of (2i+1)^2 for i in 0..n-1) = n(2n-1)(2n+1); i.e. 1^2+3^2+...+(2n-1)^2 = n(2n-1)(2n+1)/3. | proved | 2 | вҖ” | classic identities | Standard finite-sum identity вҲ‘(2k-1)^2 = n(2n-1)(2n+1)/3; Concrete Mathematics В§2.5 exercises; Gradshteyn & Ryzhik, Table of Integrals, Series, and Products (sums section). |
| `sum-range-sq-triangular-form` вҖ” For every natural n, 3В·(sum of iВІ for i in 0..n) = (sum of i for i in 0..n)В·(2n+1); i.e. вҲ‘kВІ = TВ·(2n+1)/3 where T = вҲ‘k = n(n+1)/2 is the n-th triangular number. | proved | 2 | вҖ” | classic identities (Faulhaber-in-T tower вҖ” power sums as polynomials in the triangular number) | Faulhaber's theorem: вҲ‘kбө– is a polynomial in T = n(n+1)/2; the even-power cases carry a factor (2n+1). вҲ‘kВІ = n(n+1)(2n+1)/6 = T(2n+1)/3. Knuth, "Johann Faulhaber and sums of powers", Math. Comp. 61 (1993). |
| `sum-range-three-consecutive-product` вҖ” For every natural n, 4В·вҲ‘_{i<n} i(i+1)(i+2) = (nвҲ’1)В·nВ·(n+1)В·(n+2): the telescoping sum of products of three consecutive integers (the tetrahedral-by-4 closed form). | proved | 2 | вҖ” | falling-factorial telescoping | Graham, Knuth & Patashnik, Concrete Mathematics, В§2.6 (summation by parts on falling factorials); вҲ‘_{i=1}^{m} i(i+1)(i+2) = m(m+1)(m+2)(m+3)/4. |
| `sum-sq-add-three-ge-two-sum` вҖ” For all real a,b,c, aВІ+bВІ+cВІ+3 вүҘ 2(a+b+c). | proved | 2 | вҖ” | Classic elementary inequality / number-theory fact (#400 plan Phase 3). | For all real a,b,c, aВІ+bВІ+cВІ+3 вүҘ 2(a+b+c). Not a named mathlib lemma in this form. |
| `sum-three-squares-ge-sum-products` вҖ” For all real a, b, c, the sum of squares dominates the sum of pairwise products: aВІ + bВІ + cВІ вүҘ ab + bc + ca. | proved | 2 | вҖ” | Classic three-variable inequality (a corollary of the rearrangement / sum-of-squares principle). | ab + bc + ca вүӨ aВІ + bВІ + cВІ. mathlib has `inner_mul_le_norm_mul_norm` (CauchyвҖ“Schwarz) and `sq_nonneg`, but no named three-variable "sum of squares вүҘ sum of products" lemma. |
| `thirty-dvd-pow-five-sub-self` вҖ” For every integer n, 30 вҲЈ nвҒө вҲ’ n (Fermat: 2,3,5 each divide nвҒөвҲ’n). | proved | 2 | вҖ” | Classic elementary number theory (library-growth batch, #400 plan Phase 3). | For every integer n, 30 вҲЈ nвҒө вҲ’ n (Fermat: 2,3,5 each divide nвҒөвҲ’n). mathlib has `ZMod.pow_card` (Fermat) but not these specific named divisibility lemmas. |
| `three-cubes-div-nine` вҖ” For every natural n, 9 divides n^3 + (n+1)^3 + (n+2)^3; the sum of any three consecutive cubes is divisible by 9. | proved | 2 | вҖ” | classic identities | Classic introductory number-theory / olympiad exercise; Engel, Problem-Solving Strategies (divisibility chapter); SierpiЕ„ski, Elementary Theory of Numbers (PWN/North-Holland, 1988). |
| `twelve-dvd-pow-four-sub-sq` вҖ” For every integer n, 12 вҲЈ nвҒҙ вҲ’ nВІ. | proved | 2 | вҖ” | Classic elementary inequality / number-theory fact (#400 plan Phase 3). | For every integer n, 12 вҲЈ nвҒҙ вҲ’ nВІ. Not a named mathlib lemma in this form. |

Not a named mathlib lemma in this form. |
